message:
fix(client): treat empty XDG_CONFIG_HOME as unset (XDG spec)

Both claude_desktop_config_path and default_config_paths fell into
the XDG branch when `XDG_CONFIG_HOME=""` (empty string), because
env::var returns Ok("") for an empty value, not Err. The result
was that PathBuf::from("").join("claude/config.json") produced a
relative path "claude/config.json" instead of the spec-mandated
fallback to $HOME/.config/claude/config.json.

Per XDG Base Directory Specification:
> If $XDG_CONFIG_HOME is either not set or empty, a default equal
> to $HOME/.config should be used.

Both functions now filter out empty values explicitly:

    env::var("XDG_CONFIG_HOME").ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty())

so an empty value falls through to the home-dir fallback the same
way an unset value does.

Pre-existing bug uncovered while reviewing the BSD widening fix
(975f6bc / cf7bf68); same fix applied to both functions for
consistency. No behavior change for callers who have XDG_CONFIG_HOME
set to a non-empty path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
